posts.caption:
Rule of thumb: never skip a Paul Smith campaign. Walton Goggins recently returned as the face of Paul Smith for AW26, this time getting a crash course in sartorial etiquette from Sir Paul himself.
The campaign unfolds across four short films directed by Cameron Turnbull, with stills by Samuel Bradley, set against the British countryside. Styled by Ben Schofield, it leans into everything @paulsmithdesign does best: heritage fabrics like Harris Tweed and Donegal wool, offset with just enough wit to keep it from feeling too proper.
Tie rules, the sock dilemma in shoes-off households, the “Sometimes, Always, Never” jacket principle—it’s all there, ending with Sir Paul stepping in to inspect the final look.
#PaulSmith #WaltonGoggins #FashionCampaign #MenswearStyle #Tailoring

posts.caption:
Bring me here ASAP. Built between 1902 and 1930, Lluís Domènech i Montaner designed Hospital de la Santa Creu i Sant Pau around a radical idea for its time: that sunlight, gardens, color, and beautiful architecture could actively help heal the human body.
While a modern hospital still operates next door, the original Art Nouveau pavilions have since become a UNESCO World Heritage Site: a sprawling “garden city” of tiled facades, mosaic details, palm-lined pathways, and stained glass flooded with natural light.
A genuinely therapeutic place to spend an afternoon.
#Barcelona #ArtNouveau #Architecture #Travel #TheCitizensPoste

posts.caption:
before TikTok made them viral, they were already legends. the best part? the originals are still out there, more specifically, they’re all on @vestiaireco
long before “quiet luxury,” “indie sleaze,” and “old
money” became aesthetics, Kate Moss was dragging a Balenciaga City through London, the Olsen twins were carrying Hermès like it was an afterthought, and Paris Hilton just owned your whole wishlist.
fashion’s most wanted bags rarely appear out of
nowhere. the pieces dominating wishlists today were first worn, loved, and occasionally destroyed by a generation of IT girls who made them iconic in the first place.
these are the bags that walked so your Pinterest board could run.
#TFO #fashion #VestiaireCollective #prelovedsale
posts.alt_text:
we all have that bag - the one that survives every wardrobe clean-out, every trend cycle, and somehow only gets better with time.

Bottega Veneta’s IL MIO celebrates the bond between a person and their most treasured companion, with Intrecciato icons captured by Drew Vickers under the creative direction of Louise Trotter.

proof that some bags aren’t just accessories… they’re part of the story

#TFO #bottegaveneta

posts.caption:
Fatherhood is a new role for the actor who first got noticed for his performance in ‘Masaan’ (2015). In fact, it seems this off-camera Vicky Kaushal (@vickykaushal09) is perhaps closer to Deepak Chaudhary, the character he played in the film. A sensitive, ambitious young man coming to terms with the world around him. That character, Kaushal says, changed him. But not for the obvious reasons.
“Until then success, to me, was where you reached [in life]. It was never about where you started from,” he explains. “That character, starting from the crematorium ghat in Benaras and going on to work as an engineer in the Railways in Allahabad [now Prayagraj]... He had a far bigger life arc than I’ve covered in my life. Till today,” he says, tapping the arm rest for emphasis. “After that I started having so much empathy for those around me, especially those from far less privileged backgrounds, because I don’t know where they have started from.”
@travellerprasad in conversation with the Bollywood actor at the link in bio.
Editorial Direction: @meghamahindru @ridhimasapre
Photography: @yiiooi
DOP: @jeffwangyu

posts.caption:
Thirty minutes after this interview was supposed to begin, Vicky Kaushal’s (@vickykaushal09) tall, statuesque frame bursts in through the door at the Matrix office in Khar, Mumbai. “So sorry, bro,” he says, shaking this reporter’s hand with both of his. His day has been busy—his son’s visa was to be sorted, he couldn’t leave his wife alone, and there’s repair work going on in his building, he says, sounding like your average young husband and father with his daily household chores to take care of, not some Bollywood star with a platoon of staff to handle his every whim.
Since becoming a father, Kaushal says he’s come to understand and appreciate “a lot more” of the work his parents did in raising him. “It occurs to me at the most random moments that that [instilling the right values] is now my responsibility towards my son,” he says. “He is going to learn by observing how his parents are thinking, talking, and behaving. So, I’ve realised whatever you want to see reflected in him has to come in your actions rather than words.”
@travellerprasad in conversation with the Bollywood actor at the link in bio.

posts.caption:
The Indian monsoon does not care about your wash day. One humid commute is all it takes for smooth, freshly styled hair to turn into a frizzy canopy by lunchtime, and the waxy balms and flyaway sticks stashed in your desk drawer will only make things worse. Greasy roots, crunchy mid-lengths… You know the drill.
For years, formulas in the anti-frizz category focused on flattening hair into submission, relying on heavy silicones and oils that often traded humidity protection for stringy tresses. Beauty’s obsession with sleek, pin-straight hair has also waned. The goal is no longer to erase texture but to help it survive the weather. Thankfully, today’s products work in more than just one way.
Different approaches, same brief: a barrier between your hair and whatever the sky decides to do.
At the link in bio, @byshyamolikavaz with the hair formulas worth reaching for.
@milkshakeusa @nexxushaircare @bbluntindia @beminimalist__ @brillareprofessional

posts.caption:
Project Grain(@projectgrain.blr ), the new Bengaluru bar, holds its essence in its name. When you step into the dimly-lit vault, every flicker of light celebrates the grain on the wood that surrounds you. From the patterned wooden bar frame and the unstructured wood chandelier to the corner stage waiting for a jazz band to step in, the space relies on material to do the talking. Despite being located in the plush lobby of Sheraton Grand Bangalore Hotel at Brigade Gateway, the 60-seater doubles as an escape to the ‘Mad Men’-era with a focus on leather and dark wood.
At the link in bio, @darshitaagoyal’s full review of the bar.

posts.caption:
“Everyone talks about the films and the fashion from Cannes, but no one tells you that the festival is just an excuse to soak up the delicious seaside in the South of France. With a dozen towns a quick train ride away from each other, you can scale the Riviera one Aperol spritz at a time.
Take a quick train 20 minutes north of Cannes and arrive at Antibes, a seaside town where quaint cobblestone paths sit unassuming beside luxury super yachts. Away from the tourist traps of Nice and the €€€ of Cannes, you don’t have to bank on Instagram for hidden gems—they’re all around you.
Ask me; I recently spent a long weekend in Antibes with my best friends and we didn’t want to leave the sunny coast.”
At the link in bio, @darshitaagoyal with 10 things to see, shop, do, eat, and drink over a weekend in Antibes.
@lesfillesdumicocoulier @boulangerie_veziano @antibesbooks @pastaebasta.antibes

posts.caption:
Hi, I’m Veronica 👋🏼 a sewing pattern designer based in Melbourne, Australia.
I create patterns, tools and resources that help you sew with understanding.
My work focuses on how fabrics behave, how garments are constructed, and how sewing connects us to the history of what we wear.
Sewing Patterns
My patterns are designed for versatility, wearability and fun. They include the Hazel, Freya, Odette, Audrey, Hera, Cordelia and the Sleeve Studio — each combining comfort with thoughtful design details. You’ll also find The Noelle, a free beginner-friendly pattern that introduces my approach to fit and construction.
The Paper Doll
A print-at-home paper doll designed to help you visualise and play with my sewing patterns. You can cut it out and colour it yourself, or use the pre-filled versions featuring my fabric prints and colour palettes. The set includes curvier model options and three skin tones to choose from.
The Conscious Consumer eBook
A practical guide to fabrics, sustainability and sewing with intention. It helps you understand fibre origins, weave structures and how to make long-lasting choices when you sew or buy clothes.
Digital Sewing Tools
Two paid calculators designed for precision:
• Shirring Calculator – works out fabric shrinkage and elastic thread length.
• GSM Calculator – estimates fabric weight and drape.
Available individually or as a discounted bundle.
My PDF sewing patterns are stocked digitally through @fabricgodmother , @spotlightstores , @thefoldline , @joans.finefabric and @thedrapersdaughter .
Fabric and Accessories
You can shop my fabric designs on @spoonflower and find my illustrated phone cases at @thedairy .
Everything I create is designed to help you sew with confidence, understanding and creativity.
For all enquires email us at hello@veronicatuckerthelabel.com
posts.alt_text:
I will never stop talking about this movie 🎥 

They both strongly protect their loved ones, just in different ways. And I think a lot of the miscommunication comes from the both of them trying to do the same thing but with different approaches. 

Pride and Prejudice 2005 by Joe Wright

posts.caption: About textile crafts - magical power - women: When you have a look at the old fairytales of Germany, France and Northern Europe, these stories are also filled with that pattern. In the "Nibelungenlied" (the story of Sigfried the Dragonslayer, who gets to bath in dragonsblood which makes him uninjurable besides a very small spot on his shoulder and later gets killed through that spot by Hagen von Tronje) Krimhild, Sigfrieds Wife wants to protect him and markes the spot on his tunic with red thread. Also in the beginning of the story she and her women (she is a princess, so this is her household staff) are described to make lots of clothes for the men, also to embroider them. They did it for protection, also for presenting their social status.

posts.caption:
Every culture created a goddess who presided over making, but not all of them did it the same way.
This edition introduced two categories.
The first: goddesses whose mythology is explicitly about weaving (documented, primary sources, this is what the texts actually say).
The second: goddesses that weavers chose: figures of creativity, wisdom and skill that women at the loom reached toward deliberately, even though weaving wasn’t the centre of their story.
Both categories are true, just in different ways.
And here is what I keep coming back to across all three editions of the First Makers series. You never find a GOD of weaving. Not once, across any culture. Every time, the act of making cloth belongs to the feminine divine.
That is the oldest memory we have of what women’s knowledge meant.
Full episode on The Culture of Cloth podcast 🎧 link in bio. Start there if you’re new here.
Save this and share it with someone who makes things.
Cover art: Court Ladies Preparing Newly Woven Silk, a Chinese silk painting by Emperor Huizong of Song, early 12th century.

posts.caption:
Six months of research, four in-depth online sessions and one of the oldest stories ever told 📕
I’m joining @readingsbooks Big Book Club for a deep dive into Homer’s Odyssey. It’s an online session running every Thursday from 18 June through to the Christopher Nolan film screening on 16 July.
Penelope. Athena. Circe. Helen. Nausicaa. These women have been living in my research for months and this series is the moment I get to go deeper with scholars who’ve spent their careers inside this text.
I’ll be sharing Substack reflections after each session and the first one is free, with sessions 2–5 are for paid subscribers.
Link in bio for the Readings online membership.
In partnership with @readingsbooks

Digital Sewing Tools
Two paid calculators designed for precision:
• Shirring Calculator – works out fabric shrinkage and elastic thread length.
• GSM Calculator – estimates fabric weight and drape.
Available individually or as a discounted bundle.

Stockists
My PDF sewing patterns are stocked digitally through @fabricgodmother , @spotlightstores , @thefoldline , @joans.finefabric and @thedrapersdaughter .

Fabric and Accessories
You can shop my fabric designs on @spoonflower and find my illustrated phone cases at @thedairy .

Everything I create is designed to help you sew with confidence, understanding and creativity.

For all enquires email us at hello@veronicatuckerthelabel.com

posts.caption:
Is every influencer invited on the @Alo superyacht? From @KylieJenner to @AlixEarle, everyone is aboard Alo Voyage — a 72-meter yacht that set sail from the French Riviera in May and now doubles as the brand’s floating campaign HQ. "The idea was never about spectacle for its own sake, but to bring an elevated expression of wellness to life in a way that felt authentic to the season and our community," says Alo EVP of marketing and creative @SummerBarry_.
At the link in bio, @JosephDBobowicz explores how luxury is shifting from ownership to experience — and how brands are taking to the seas to build aspirational travel and wellness activations.
posts.alt_text: The #FIFAWorldCup kicked off with a noticeable surge in bright pink boots, cementing pink's place as a standout color on the pitch. For #SS27, we expect pops of color to dominate menswear off the field, too. "Modern masculinity sometimes involves showing nonchalance toward gender expectations. It signals individuality and openness, and that’s becoming a style statement on its own," says Frida Tordhag, fashion analyst at Heuritech. The trend is clear, but will consumers buy in? Tap the link in bio to read more on pink’s potential.

In between the peaceful mountains of Ladakh sits a restaurant inspired by its snowcapped peaks. At Ryu Oka (@ryuoka.ladakh), however, you move from dish to dish, fork to chopsticks, condiment to seasoning and realise boundary lines only exist on maps and in geography textbooks. In real life, we’re living on a spectrum, our colours and cultures as easily intertwined as the flavours on our plates.
Words by: Tyrel Rodricks (@tyrelr)
Images courtesy of Ryu Oka (@ryuoka.ladakh) and Getty Images (@gettyimages)

posts.caption:
Chaos is something @GracieAbrams has grown accustomed to. Eight years ago, she was a first-year student at Barnard with a cult fandom she’d fostered through Instagram; soon she was signed to Interscope, then on her first tour, then opening for Olivia Rodrigo and Taylor Swift. By the end of 2024, Abrams was a Grammy-nominated pop star filling arenas.
She finally took a breather last summer. “It’s not until you stop that you realize ‘Oh, I haven’t seen my friends in a long time. I haven’t cooked a meal in a long time,’” Abrams says. “The sleep schedule becomes regular for the first time in, like, four years.”
Now 26, she’s shifting into a new gear. Abrams’s third studio album, “Daughter from Hell,” out in July, sounds like her—earnest, introspective, folk-informed—but the production is more lush (“It feels like me in progress,” she says); she’s also in a happy relationship and making her first film, “Please,” with the filmmaker Halina Reijn.
For Vogue’s Summer 2026 issue, Brittany Spanos meets Gracie Abrams in Los Angeles and New York to talk about growing up, slowing down, falling in love, and where she’d like to go next. Tap the link in our bio to read the full profile.
Photographed by @larissahofmann, Written by @brittanyspanos, Styled by @jordenbickham, Hair by @tamastuzes, Makeup by @emikaneko, Manicure by @1.800.nailme, Tailoring by Irina Tshartaryan for Susie’s Custom Designs, Produced by @hyperion.la, Set Design by  @patienceharding. Shot on location @lucasmuseum. Vogue, Summer 2026.
posts.alt_text: Talk about a full circle moment. Today, @aliciakeys took the stage at the city's celebration of the @NYKnicks to perform her hit, "Empire State of Mind."

posts.caption: It's a family affair in Chicago today where the Obama family is celebrating the opening of the Barack Obama Presidential Center. Former first daughters Malia and Sasha joined their parents at the South Side museum and library, where they eschewed the prim and proper style that defined their White House childhoods. Showing off the distinct styles that they have cultivated as adults, tap the link in our bio for details on how the Obamas revamped first family style today.

posts.caption: There has been an indescribable joy sweeping New York City. Over the weekend, the @nyknicks took home their first NBA championship in 53 years, and one such die-hard enthusiast is undoubtedly New York City’s Mayor Zohran Mamdani. For today’s parade celebrating the victory, the first lady of New York City, Rama Duwaji, stepped out in a cascading Knicks T-shirt dress. “I could not be more proud of our city and everyone representing it right now,” said designer @MissClaireSullivan, who made the upcycled dress entirely from bootleg Knicks merch. Tap the link in our bio for all of the details.

posts.caption:
From Austin-based designer Daizy López alongside her family to model Erin Wasson—who hails from Irving, Texas, just northwest of Dallas and members of the Community of Faith Church, to honor our Summer issue’s focus on American narratives, this spring we embarked on a sprawling mission to document the communities and characters of modern Texas. Learn more about those whose lives and work reflect the evolving spirit of the nation at the link in our bio.
Photographed by @tylersphotos, Styled by @mr_carlos_nazario, Hair by @lucas.a.wilson, Makeup by @michaelabosch, Tailoring by @afunshephartion, Produced by @dayinternational, Local Scouting by @lilacperez, Vogue, Summer 2026.
posts.alt_text:
Chaos is something @GracieAbrams has grown accustomed to. Eight years ago, she was a first-year student at Barnard with a cult fandom she’d fostered through Instagram; soon she was signed to Interscope, then on her first tour, then opening for Olivia Rodrigo and Taylor Swift. By the end of 2024, Abrams was a Grammy-nominated pop star filling arenas.

She finally took a breather last summer. “It’s not until you stop that you realize ‘Oh, I haven’t seen my friends in a long time. I haven’t cooked a meal in a long time,’” Abrams says. “The sleep schedule becomes regular for the first time in, like, four years.”

Now 26, she’s shifting into a new gear. Abrams’s third studio album, “Daughter from Hell,” out in July, sounds like her—earnest, introspective, folk-informed—but the production is more lush (“It feels like me in progress,” she says); she’s also in a happy relationship and making her first film, “Please,” with the filmmaker Halina Reijn.

For Vogue’s Summer 2026 issue, Brittany Spanos meets Gracie Abrams in Los Angeles and New York to talk about growing up, slowing down, falling in love, and where she’d like to go next. Tap the link in our bio to read the full profile. 

Photographed by @larissahofmann, Written by @brittanyspanos, Styled by @jordenbickham, Hair by @tamastuzes, Makeup by @emikaneko, Manicure by @1.800.nailme, Tailoring by Irina Tshartaryan for Susie’s Custom Designs, Produced by @hyperion.la, Set Design by  @patienceharding. Shot on location @lucasmuseum. Vogue, Summer 2026.

posts.caption:
Alondra Acosta is a third-generation charra. Every year, the Charras de Agua Santa organize a tournament, Torneo Lauro Iván Acosta, in honor of her late father. Here, members of Charras de Agua Santa, an Austin-based escaramuza team — think an all-women rodeo, but with an emphasis on precision and style rather than speed—ride in formation in their competition dresses.
To honor our Summer issue’s focus on American narratives, this spring we embarked on a sprawling mission to document the communities and characters of modern Texas whose lives and work reflect the evolving spirit of the nation. Tap the link in our bio to see more.
Photographed by @tylersphotos, Styled by @mr_carlos_nazario, Hair by @lucas.a.wilson, Makeup by @michaelabosch, Tailoring by @afunshephartion, Produced by @dayinternational, Local Scouting by @lilacperez, Vogue, Summer 2026.

posts.caption: There has been an indescribable joy sweeping New York City. Over the weekend, the @nyknicks took home their first NBA championship in 53 years, and one such die-hard enthusiast is undoubtedly New York City’s Mayor Zohran Mamdani. For today’s parade celebrating the victory, the first lady of New York City, Rama Duwaji, stepped out in a cascading Knicks T-shirt dress. “I could not be more proud of our city and everyone representing it right now,” said designer @MissClaireSullivan, who made the upcycled dress entirely from bootleg Knicks merch. Tap the link in our bio for all of the details.

posts.caption: The experiment Alessandro Michele is conducting for resort at Valentino involves reconciling the lofty spirit of couture with the mundane demands of everyday dressing, a task that, as the Italians would say, is like mixing the devil with holy water. Yet improbable pairings have always been his forte, as have the reactions they tend to provoke. Shot in a grand Lombard villa, the collection images conjure a vision that feels like a sort of "Saltburn" transplanted to the banks of the Navigli River: decadent, blasé, and suggestive of a slightly irritating teenage debauchery. See every look from the resort 2027 collection for @maisonvalentino and read the review from @tiziana_cardini at the link in bio.

Tap the link in our bio for all of the details.

posts.caption: Italy may be out of contention at the World Cup, but one thing that they’re certainly in is fashion month. The spring 2027 menswear season kicks off in Florence with the Pitti Uomo fair, a sprawling tradeshow held at Fortezza da Basso, where brands present their latest wares to peacocking dandies. This time around, Dover Street Market’s Kei Ninomiya is one of the guest designers alongside Simone Rocha, who will be showcasing her first solo menswear show. See more of the best street style from this season at the link in bio. Photographed by @styledumonde

posts.caption:
Eight years after his first tenure at @Celine, where he served as design director under Phoebe Philo, Michael Rider returned to the house—and in the time since, has delivered three acclaimed collections. His coveted spring line is in stores, and his fall collection received a rapturous reception when it was shown in March. Rider has certainly put a stop to any lingering questions as to how a curious kid from Washington, DC, ended up running one of the most quintessentially Parisian of fashion houses. Head to the link in bio for more on how Rider is remaking the French label with a unique mix of joy, optimism, and hit accessories.
Photographed by @annieleibovitz, Written by @christbollen, Styled by @jackborkett, Grooming by @jillianhalouska, Set Design by @maryhoward_setdesign, Produced by AL Studio. Vogue, Summer 2026.

posts.caption:
Simone Rocha! Thom Browne! A Ralph Lauren return! The @PittiUomo_Official trade fair and @MilanFashionWeek Men’s show schedules this season are enhanced by some very exciting guest designers. At the link in bio, @Lucy_Maguire_ shares the brands and events to note.
Photo: @styledumonde
posts.alt_text:
Is every influencer invited on the @Alo superyacht? From @KylieJenner to @AlixEarle, everyone is aboard Alo Voyage — a 72-meter yacht that set sail from the French Riviera in May and now doubles as the brand’s floating campaign HQ. "The idea was never about spectacle for its own sake, but to bring an elevated expression of wellness to life in a way that felt authentic to the season and our community," says Alo EVP of marketing and creative @SummerBarry_.

At the link in bio, @JosephDBobowicz explores how luxury is shifting from ownership to experience — and how brands are taking to the seas to build aspirational travel and wellness activations.

posts.caption: As we start to look for our warm-weather pieces, we’re asking ourselves, “How would the Olsen twins prepare for the season?” They would let proportion do the talking, with oversized cotton poplin blouses, languid trousers, and flouncy skirts. In addition to laid-back flops, they might also pull out an unexpected pair of crochet ballet flats or beaded sandals that embody the essence of the season. The key? Start with the basics and build from there. Tap the link in our bio to shop and recreate their chicest looks.

posts.caption: One of the silver linings of being a frequent traveler—beyond the miles accrued—is that packing isn’t so much a chore as it is muscle memory. “I have it down to a science,” says Ilona Hamer, co-founder of Australian resortwear label Matteau of her dialed system. Last year alone, the stylist and consultant spent about 250 days on the go. At the link in bio, for the latest installment of #MyPackingStyle, Hamer shares what she packs for a holiday in the sun.

posts.caption: If summer’s sandals trends share a common thread, it’s a refusal to choose between any one aesthetic. Designers have traded the exclusively minimalist mood of recent summers for options that encourage self-expression. Whether it’s Le Monde Béryl’s pale blue beaded sandals, Gucci’s sporty heeled thongs, or Ancient Greek Sandals’ jelly flops, the chicest sandal trends of 2026 are personal rather than prescriptive. Discover them all at the link in bio.

posts.alt_text:
A stage. A story. A Rajkumari. 

RAJKUMARI is back, MY BIGGEST INDIA TOUR YET. (If you don’t see your city don’t worry, more cities to be added). 
Tickets on Book My Show.

What is Rajkumari?

An evening of stories, poems dipped in music. This is not just a show. It’s a gentle rebellion. Against everything you were told to be.A space to see differently.
To live differently. Your way. A reminder of the power of your own perspective, of your “Nayab Nazariya”.

After touring in 6 countries, Rajkumari is back in India. Come for one of India’s biggest storytelling & Poetry show.

Tickets on @bookmyshowin , link in my bio🔗

✨ Creator, Actor, PHENOMENON! ✨ On the June-July cover of Wedding Vows, we present the trailblazing @mostlysane, Prajakta Koli!
From building a YouTube empire to being the lead for Netflix Original Mismatched, from being part of Michelle Obama’s Day Time Emmy winning docu-series to being UNDP India's First Youth Climate Champion, her journey is nothing short of extraordinary.
With her debut romance novel set to launch this year, along with her first Amazon Prime Original Andhera, Prajakta’s journey is a testament to the power of dreams, hard work, and staying true to oneself. She continues to inspire millions with her authenticity, relatability, and unstoppable spirit.

posts.caption:
@camimendes is having one of the biggest years in her career so far. A major summer blockbuster, the second feature film from her production company, and wedding planning are all on the roster for 2026. This month, Mendes steps into the sprawling world of Masters of the Universe as the sword-slinging Teela alongside Nicholas Galitzine, Idris Elba, and Jared Leto.
The project is a stretch from her days as Veronica Lodge in #Riverdale, which catapulted her into mainstream recognition nearly a decade ago. Mendes makes movies for “the girls and the gays,” she states lovingly, but her star power is only going to get bigger and brighter with Masters. “I’ve never been a part of something of this scale,” she says of the project.
In our Now Playing Issue, presented by @bvlgari, we chat with the actress about reinventing herself in this era of her life. Read it all in the full interview at the link in our bio.

producer: @_porcelainchaos @delafe_pr

posts.alt_text:
There would be no Pope Heyward without @jonathandavissofficial. Much like his smart, careful, deeply moral Outer Banks character, Daviss knows what it feels like to carry the pressure of proving your worth and refusing to let yourself or anyone else down. “What really stood out to me about Pope in season 1 was this idea of expectations and pressure that gets put on a lot of young Black teens and teens in general,” he says. “There’s this pressure to succeed, and it was something I truly have been through.”

Daviss still thinks back to his early audition days before landing the life-changing role on OBX, when the constant need to prove himself as a young actor felt emotionally exhausting. His mindset then? “You only lose when you quit.”

Head to the link in our bio for the full download in our Now Playing Issue presented by @bvlgari, from what to expect from the fifth and final season of OBX to his upcoming portrayal of a young Snoop Dogg.

posts.caption:
@victoriamonet is trading her claws for butterfly wings. The three-time Grammy winner is the definition of a multi-hyphenate: a culinary school graduate, a mother, and, of course, a singer-songwriter with a nearly two-decade-spanning catalog to her name. Now, using her 37th birthday as a launching pad, Monét has cleared her mental space and scrubbed her socials to prepare for her next chapter—an album she’s dubbed her very own Thriller, a sonically surprising, genre-defying project that marks a softer evolution. “The jaguar exists within me always, but I’m feeling like a softer version of myself now,” she says.
After all, Monét’s critically acclaimed debut studio album Jaguar II was the ultimate learning lesson. “Once you have an accolade like that, people often look for a reason to discredit why you got it,” she explains. “I think—pending a Grammy happens again—I would do the opposite and bunker down in the studio.”
Step inside Monét’s world in our Now Playing Issue, presented by @bvlgari, at the link in our bio.

posts.caption:
“The vision was simple but intentional: one big vacation with all of our favorite people,” @alexisluzfelman tells us of her wedding to Andrew. Following a private vow celebration in the Philippines, the couple privatized the oceanfront Hotel Christopher in Saint Bart’s for 100 of their closest friends. “We wanted our guests to truly experience Saint Bart’s—its beauty, French flair, vibrant energy, and festive spirit,” Alexis says.
Alongside golden sunsets, salty ocean air, and friends coming together to celebrate love, Alexis’s bridal wardrobe became a defining part of the weekend’s visual story. A collection of five archival looks (think 2009 Ralph Lauren and Phoebe Philo–era Chloé), a custom two-piece skirt set from @lein_studio, and a brand-new @colleenallen chiffon dress sealed the weekend in style. See all of the details in our latest #WhoWhatWearWeddings feature at the link in our bio.

posts.caption:
"My years of making YouTube videos have really taught me how to tell a story, both in a small way and also in a big-picture way," @claudiasulewski tells us.
The YouTuber turned beauty-brand founder is celebrating @cyklarofficial’s third anniversary this fall. The multisensory bodycare brand has hit milestone after milestone, including a Who What Wear Beauty 100 award and Marie Claire's 2025 Prix d'Excellence Award. Reflecting on her early YouTube content-creation days as BeyondBeautyStar, Sulewski says every step along the way has prepared her for launching Cyklar. “So much of owning a brand is telling a story through your product launches, through your marketing, your imagery, everything."
We chat with Sulewski about building her brand and everything else she’s dreaming up. Read the full interview at the link in our bio.

The project is a stretch from her days as Veronica Lodge in #Riverdale, which catapulted her into mainstream recognition nearly a decade ago. Mendes makes movies for “the girls and the gays,” she states lovingly, but her star power is only going to get bigger and brighter with Masters. “I’ve never been a part of something of this scale,” she says of the project. 

In our Now Playing Issue, presented by @bvlgari, we chat with the actress about reinventing herself in this era of her life. Read it all in the full interview at the link in our bio.

posts.caption:
Malo FW26 collection explores the balance between structure and ease.
Timeless knitwear, refined textures and a calm palette come together in a wardrobe designed to be worn effortlessly.
@maloitalia
Discover more at #Linkinbio
posts.alt_text: Inside @simonerocha_’s menswear debut runway show at @pittiuomo_official. WWD Milan Business and Fashion editor @martinocarrera and Style Director @thealexbadia give us their Quick Take on the spring 2027 collection and what Rocha had to say prior to the show.

posts.caption:
Jonathan Anderson has officially joined the pantheon of creative directors featured at @Dior’s permanent exhibition space in Paris.
A large portrait of the Irish designer wearing a gray marl T-shirt now welcomes visitors in room three of La Galerie Dior. It sits alongside photos of the previous seven couturiers who have helmed the French fashion house, going all the way back to founder Christian Dior.
Each image is accompanied by a signature design: in Anderson’s case, the black Rêve coat with a swooping collar from his debut haute couture collection presented in January.
Read more at WWD.com — #linkinbio
Report: @jdiderich
posts.alt_text: Inside @simonerocha_’s menswear debut runway show at @pittiuomo_official. WWD Milan Business and Fashion editor @martinocarrera and Style Director @thealexbadia give us their Quick Take on the spring 2027 collection and what Rocha had to say prior to the show.

posts.caption:
Uni (@weareuni) has named @KaiaGerber its first Creative Partner in Residence, as the Los Angeles-based beauty brand expands into more than 1,600 @UltaBeauty doors nationwide on July 12.
Gerber, the 24-year-old model and actress, will collaborate across campaigns, partnerships, events and product development, though her involvement remains in the “discovery phase,” said Uni founder and chief executive officer Alexandra Keating.
“Everything’s kind of on the table,” Keating said. “We’ve just got to figure out what’s right for her and us, and where we think that there’s opportunity.”
Read more at WWD.com — #linkinbio
Report: @ryma___________
posts.alt_text: Inside @simonerocha_’s menswear debut runway show at @pittiuomo_official. WWD Milan Business and Fashion editor @martinocarrera and Style Director @thealexbadia give us their Quick Take on the spring 2027 collection and what Rocha had to say prior to the show.

posts.caption:
A mischievous vibe ran through the new collection @alessandro_michele designed for @maisonvalentino, with the cheeky attitude spelled out loud and clear via the words “Villain Teen” that occasionally popped up printed on leotards or embroidered on baseball caps. A pun on the brand’s name and a nod to Michele’s own “enfant terrible” inclination, the motto is “a bit of an invitation to transgression through beauty” and to “maintain a childlike heart, but also a bit irreverent,” said the designer in a preview.
“Who knows if I’m talking about myself unconsciously? The other day I was thinking that I’m a bit of a gentle villain, in the sense that I do as I please,” he quipped. “But I think it’s nice to restore a certain playfulness and lightness into something that lived of sumptuousness and distance.”
Hence the resort 2027 collection, where Michele mixed high and low in offering his take on preppy style and the house’s polished codes. The motto pieces were combined with tailored pleated pants; bon ton boxy jackets were layered over fleece sweatshirts and denim skirts; collegiate striped shirts and cardigans or delicate see-through blouses were tucked into joggers, while tracksuits seamlessly coexisting with opulent sequined jackets were grounded in high-heeled suede boots or the new Rockstuds pumps.
Read more at WWD.com — #linkinbio
Report: @sandrasalibian
posts.alt_text: Inside @simonerocha_’s menswear debut runway show at @pittiuomo_official. WWD Milan Business and Fashion editor @martinocarrera and Style Director @thealexbadia give us their Quick Take on the spring 2027 collection and what Rocha had to say prior to the show.

posts.caption:
It’s a moment London has been waiting for since 2009 and now it has finally arrived in a blaze of color, and with a celebration of British tradition, art and craft.
On Friday, @Hermès will open its London flagship at 166 New Bond Street, former home of British jeweler Asprey, 17 years after buying the property and following a six-year refurbishment overseen by artistic director Pierre-Alexis Dumas and his team.
It is the sixth Hermès flagship worldwide, and the opening is a defiant act of optimism in a city plagued by financial challenges that include sky-high taxes on businesses, the repeal of duty-free shopping for foreign tourists and the flight of the rich following the election of a Labour government two years ago.
Read more at WWD.com — #linkinbio
Report: @samanthavconti

posts.caption:
L’Oréal is tackling what it calls “the 11-minute paradox.”
“We can be more demanding of AI,” said Asmita Dubey, L’Oréal’s chief digital and marketing officer, explaining it can be used to augment the consumer’s journey, as well as the marketer’s.
The paradox is emerging in the consumer’s journey, she said, speaking prior to her presentation at VivaTech, the technology conference in Paris being held on Wednesday, where L’Oréal is unveiling a major collaboration with OpenAI.
Read more at WWD.com — #linkinbio
Report: @jenniferbethweil
